{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":558577,"duration_api_ms":558781,"ttft_ms":4411,"num_turns":69,"result":"Review complete. **Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** — CP00-784 is eligible to close.

The descriptor-only pack's production_ready claim is fully supported: distribution matches the plan exactly (150 units), the implementation/validators/tests enforce descriptor-only + no-write + no-real-data + runtime-closed boundaries, all 27 evidence commands pass (exit 0), the CP178+ review hardening correctly rejects invalid evidence, and the authority boundary holds (Claude read-only/non-final; Hermes control-plane metadata only). No overclaim — manifest/inspection keep `production_ready:false` pending this review, exactly as designed.

One **non-blocking P3** noted: `changed-file-scope.json` was snapshotted before the plan/queue regeneration, so the two dirty plan files (`closeout-pack-plan.json`, `next-pack-queue.json`) tied to CP00-784 aren't captured. Provenance artifact only; no gate depends on it.

(4) validators.js:2522/2550 enforce distribution, section rows, descriptor-only, no-leak guards, Hermes/Claude gates, read-only Claude packet, and handoff; (5) model.test.js:1065-1130 asserts coverage.errors=[], descriptor validation errors=[], runtime-closed flags, hermes.emits_runtime_receipt=false, claude.read_only=true, handoff to CP00-785, contract latest_projection=cp784 descriptor, and contract validation.valid=true; (6) command-evidence.json records 27 commands all exit_code 0, including all 10 required validation commands plus git diff --check, with no failures; (7) the CP178+ review hardening library (scripts/lib/closeout-pack-claude-review-hardening.mjs) correctly rejects auth failures, 0-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced JSON/prose, tool-call-shaped output, missing required fields, permission denials, and missing structured_output, and gates closeout eligibility on PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS with p0/p1/p2=0; (8) Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer (not final approver), Hermes is used only as control-plane descriptor metadata (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; no spawn/exec/fetch/http in product code), and no secrets/PII are present. No overclaim: manifest production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false, construction-inspection verdict=PENDING with descriptor_verified=false, all correctly gated on this review; command-evidence.production_ready=true is the established intra-pack command-gate field (identical pattern to closed CP00-783).
